justification: All pass criteria met: Authentication per consumer type (JWT for mobile, API key for partners) correctly designed with dedicated Auth Service. Internal traffic separation fully addressed with separate Internal Gateway on private network with mTLS. Business logic explicitly excluded from gateway (multiple mentions in boundaries, pitfalls_to_avoid). SPOF risk identified for both Auth Service and gateway misconfiguration. Webhook delivery for partners addressed with dedicated Webhook Dispatcher including retry, dead letter queue, and HMAC signatures. flexibility_points (5) and rigidity_points (4) present and well-reasoned. Technology recommendation includes build/buy consideration (Kong, Envoy, AWS API Gateway, custom) with acknowledgment that choice depends on team expertise.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough gateway design with three distinct ingress paths and clear separation of edge vs internal traffic
Explicit and repeated emphasis on keeping business logic out of the gateway, with authorization delegated to downstream services
Identity header spoofing risk identified with defense-in-depth mitigation at both gateway and service layers
Webhook Dispatcher fully designed as async system with exponential backoff, dead letter queue, HMAC signatures, and partner dashboard
Phased migration strategy reduces blast radius with rollback-safe increments
Rate limiter fail-open vs fail-closed trade-off explicitly discussed with rationale
Technology choices presented as options with trade-offs rather than prescriptive recommendation of a single product

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"The logistics platform needs a unified API gateway layer to replace inconsistent per-service auth and rate limiting. The design introduces a tiered gateway architecture with three distinct ingress paths (mobile, partner, internal) sharing a common gateway core but with channel-specific policies. This eliminates duplicated auth logic from downstream services, centralizes rate limiting, and enforces network-level isolation for internal traffic. The architecture uses a single
